title: T-028 — API + worker: D365 optional import/export sync job with idempotency
prompt: Per §13.3 + §13.5 + V-TEC-70..73. Implement POST /api/technical/d365/sync (manual trigger) that enqueues a d365_sync_jobs row + cron handler `dailyD365Pull` (lib/integrations/d365/pull.ts). Worker calls D365 Data Entity API `/data/Products` and `/data/BOMVersions/BOMLines` filtered by ModifiedDateTime > last_sync. Prepare import diffs for items / bom_headers / bom_lines via match on d365_item_id; apply overwrite only through an authorized workflow/person. Drift detection: when local items.updated_at > d365_last_sync_at AND incoming differs → audit_log action='d365_drift', skip overwrite (V-TEC-73). Refuse when integration.d365.enabled=false (412 V-TEC-70). Idempotency_key = hash(entity+record_key+direction+payload_version). RED tests/wiring/d365-pull.test.ts using msw to mock D365. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/d365-pull.test.ts`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Optional D365 integration worker with idempotency, diff/audit, and authorized overwrite-only semantics.
pipeline_inputs.details: Use msw or nock to mock D365 endpoints in tests. Cron registration in lib/cron/registry.ts. Connection config from 02-SETTINGS §11.3 (encrypted via pgcrypto). Failure path writes to d365_sync_dlq. D365 is integration only; Monopilot-owned data is canonical. Pull/import may stage diffs but must not silently overwrite local product/BOM/WIP data.
acceptance_criteria: Given integration.d365.enabled=false, when POST /sync runs, then 412 V-TEC-70.

Given local Monopilot data differs from incoming D365 data, when worker processes record, then audit row 'd365_drift' or an import-diff row is written and canonical Monopilot rows are not overwritten without authorized workflow.
Given duplicate idempotency_key, when worker runs again, then second attempt is no-op.
Given mocked D365 returns 3 items modified, when pull completes, then d365_sync_jobs.records_processed=3.
test_strategy: RED tests using msw/nock for V-TEC-70..73 paths.
risk_red_lines: Do not call live D365 in tests.
Do not store credentials in plaintext.
Do not make D365 the canonical source of Technical master data; no silent overwrite/import from D365.
